title: Design System Documentation Spec (DSDS) v0.15.1
description: The design system documentation spec (DSDS) is machine-readable format for design system documentation. Each DSDS document is either an entity (a document for a specific design token, component, pattern, etc.) or an entity group (collection of entities). Each entity has a `kind` property that defines its subject matter. DSDS entities support component, token, token-group, theme, foundation, pattern, guide, or chunk. Entity schemas are in entities/. Document block schemas are in document-blocks/. Common schemas are in common/.

$schema.description: URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation.

dsdsVersion.description: The DSDS spec version this document follows.

extends.description: Declares that this DSDS document inherits from another DSDS document. This is typically used for systems with a core/extension model. The base document provides the core entities. This document layers on top, adding or extending entities.

documentBlocks.description: Structured documentation for the design system as a whole. Examples include defining the system's purpose (when to adopt the system), system-level guidelines, and overviews. It's accepts general document blocks: `use-cases`, `guidelines`, `sections`, `accessibility`, `content`, or `checklist`.

entityGroups.description: One or more entity groups. Each group is named with a single `entities` array. That array holds any kind of entity — components, tokens, token groups, themes, foundations, patterns, guides, and chunks. The order of the array represents the default display order. Multiple groups let one DSDS file organize entities into logical sections (ex:, one group for foundations, another for components). Groups can be inlined or referenced through a $ref object that points to another DSDS file.

entity.description: The entity information for an individual component, token, token group, theme, foundation, pattern, guide, or chunk. Use this instead of `entityGroups` when each entity lives in its own file. The `kind` field on the entity identifies its type.

anyEntity.description: The required entity `kind` property identifies its type and selects its schema via one `if`/`then` branch per kind. This checks that an entity is referencing the correct schema.

fileRef.description: A JSON Reference to a DSDS entity or entity group defined in another file. Tooling ( json-schema-ref-parser) resolves the reference before validation and bundling. The URI should include a JSON Pointer fragment to target a specific value within the referenced file (ex: './button.dsds.json#/entity', './groups/tokens.dsds.json#/entityGroups/0').

$ref.description: A relative reference to a DSDS file, optionally with a JSON Pointer fragment that identifies the target value within that file (ex: './button.dsds.json#/entity'). The value MUST be relative. The pattern rejects absolute paths, protocol-relative references, and scheme-bearing URIs (http:, file:, …). A reference to another system uses `extends.url`. Resolvers MUST detect cycles. They MUST treat unresolvable files or pointers and shape mismatches as fatal. They MUST gate any remote dereferencing behind an allow-list (see the spec's reference-resolution and security rules).

entityGroup.description: A named collection of design system entities. All entities live in a single `entities` array, in display order, mixing entity types freely. Each entity's required `kind` identifies its type, so no per-type arrays are needed. A group MUST hold at least one entity.

name.description: Human-readable name of the collection (ex: 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation'). This acts as display label only.

entities.description: The group's entities, in order of default display. A group can contain any mix of entity kinds. Items may be inlined or referenced through a $ref object that points to another DSDS file. Order is significant: tools SHOULD keep it for display.
